methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Matic Robotics is a Series A-stage consumer technology company founded by Navneet Dalal, Mehul Nariyawala, and Shafaq Presswala. The company has raised $107M to date. The relevant peer set consists of three private-stage consumer tech companies: Shinkei, Daylight Computer, and Whisper Aero. No direct public comparables were identified in the frontier tech consumer space, limiting traditional trading multiples analysis.

The Stage-Progression method was the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($107M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage progressions yields an implied valuation of $20M. This methodology reflects normalized step-up dynamics within the consumer tech stage progression framework, though the substantial prior raise relative to this implied post-money suggests either significant capital deployment against modest asset creation or potential market headwinds since deployment.

The triangulated valuation range is $17M (low) to $23M (median $20M). The narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than methodological triangulation across revenue, comps, or DCF approaches. Key uncertainties include lack of public comparables to establish sector-level valuation precedent, absence of private peer valuation data to calibrate the step-up multiplier, and limited visibility into revenue traction or unit economics that would anchor fundamental analysis. Investors should prioritize obtaining peer round data and clarifying capital deployment metrics before finalizing valuation.
